CREATE TABLE test_intervals (id INTEGER, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_intervals VALUES (1, INTERVAL '14 months');
INSERT INTO test_intervals VALUES (2, INTERVAL '2 years');
INSERT INTO test_intervals VALUES (3, INTERVAL '1 year 6 months');
INSERT INTO test_intervals VALUES (4, INTERVAL '1 day');
SELECT datepart('year', duration) FROM test_intervals WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, datepart('month', duration) AS month_part FROM test_intervals WHERE duration = INTERVAL '14 months';
SELECT id, datepart('day', duration) AS day_part FROM test_intervals WHERE duration < INTERVAL '2 years';
CREATE TABLE additional_data (extra_id INTEGER, extra_duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (1, INTERVAL '3 months');
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (2, INTERVAL '6 months');

SELECT a.id, datepart('year', a.duration) AS year_part, b.extra_id, datepart('month', b.extra_duration) AS extra_month_part FROM test_intervals AS a JOIN additional_data AS b ON datepart('month', a.duration) = datepart('month', b.extra_duration);
SELECT datepart('year', INTERVAL '18 months') + datepart('month', INTERVAL '18 months') AS total_parts;
SELECT a.id, datepart('year', a.duration) + datepart('month', a.duration) AS total_year_month FROM test_intervals AS a WHERE datepart('year', a.duration) > 0;
SELECT datepart('month', INTERVAL '1 year 0 months') AS month_part;
SELECT id, datepart('year', duration) FROM test_intervals WHERE duration = INTERVAL '0 years';
SELECT datepart('month', INTERVAL '2 years 3 months') AS total_months;
SELECT (SELECT datepart('month', duration) FROM test_intervals WHERE id = 2) + (SELECT datepart('day', duration) FROM test_intervals WHERE id = 3) AS combined_parts;
